permuteDE 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference

Calculate number of possible group combinations

Source: R/countCombinations.R
countCombinations.Rd

For a set of replicates that belong to two groups, this function will calculate how many different randomized group combinations are possible (i.e., how many ways to divide the replicates into two groups while retaining the same number of replicates per group). The input should be either the replicate and group column names from an object's metadata, or simply the total number of replicates (n) and the number of samples in one of the two groups (k).

countCombinations(
  object = NULL,
  replicate_labels = NULL,
  group_labels = NULL,
  use_cells = NULL,
  n_replicates = NULL,
  n_group1 = NULL
)

Arguments

object

An object of class 'Seurat' or 'SingleCellExperiment'.

replicate_labels

A character string or vector indicating the name of the column containing the replicate labels. Defaults to NULL.

group_labels

A character string or vector indicating the name of the column containing the two comparison group labels. Defaults to NULL.

use_cells

A vector of cell names subset to. Default = NULL will use all cells.

n_replicates

A numeric value indicating the total number of replicates. Defaults to NULL.

n_group1

A numeric value indicating the number of replicates that belong to one of the two groups (doesn't matter which). Defaults to NULL.

Value

Returns a numeric value indicating the number of possible group combinations.

Details

The formula is: C = n!/(k!(n-k)!) (see base function choose)

Contents

Developed by Cathrine Petersen.
